# Master Decision goldens (M1.24)

12 named golden fixtures locking the `DailyDecision` wire shape produced by
`engine.decision.produce_daily_decision()`. Per plan v1.2 §19 Phase 1 Done
bar (`12/12 golden DailyDecision snapshots match`) and the M1.24 dev spec
(`docs/phased-design/phase-1/m1.24-master-decision-goldens.md`).

## Each fixture is a directory containing

- `README.md` — 1-paragraph scenario description + plan refs + intended emit.
- `inputs.json` — canonical-JSON form of the kwargs passed to
  `produce_daily_decision()`.
- `expected.json` — canonical-JSON form of the `DailyDecision` produced by
  running the engine on those inputs. Generated by
  `packages/engine/scripts/regenerate_decision_goldens.py`; reviewed manually
  on first generation, then locked.

## Replay

```bash
cd packages/engine
uv run pytest tests/test_master_decision_goldens.py -q
```

Each fixture runs as a separate parametrized test case
(`test_golden_decision_matches[NN-slug]`); the harness compares the
serialized engine output against the committed `expected.json` byte-for-byte.

## Regeneration

After an intentional schema change to `DailyDecision`, or when authoring a
new fixture's `inputs.json`:

```bash
cd packages/engine
uv run python scripts/regenerate_decision_goldens.py --all
# or for a single fixture:
uv run python scripts/regenerate_decision_goldens.py --fixture 01-high-iv-event-collar-feasible
```

Review the resulting `git diff` carefully before committing. The
`test_regenerate_decision_goldens_idempotent.py` test will catch
nondeterminism; the meta tests in
`test_master_decision_goldens_meta.py` will catch coverage regressions.

## Scenario matrix

The 12 fixtures cover all 8 V1 emit codes (per plan §22.8), both M1.11b collar
paths (feasible + degraded), both M1.12 ladder outcomes (rescued + escalated),
and all 6 ADR-0002 regimes.

| # | Fixture | Regime | M1.9 rule | Emit | M1.11b | M1.12 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 01 | `01-high-iv-event-collar-feasible` | `HIGH_IV_EVENT` | `open_collar_pre_event` | `OPEN_COLLAR` | structure present | no |
| 02 | `02-high-iv-event-collar-no-feasible-pair` | `HIGH_IV_EVENT` | `open_collar_pre_event` | `OPEN_COLLAR` | structure=None | no |
| 03 | `03-high-iv-pin-sell-call` | `HIGH_IV_PIN` | `high_iv_sell_call` | `SELL_COVERED_CALL_PARTIAL` | N/A | no |
| 04 | `04-low-iv-trend-buy-put` | `LOW_IV_TREND` | `buy_long_dated_put_low_iv_trend` | `BUY_LONG_DATED_PUT` | N/A | no |
| 05 | `05-low-iv-trend-income-overrides-buy-put` | `LOW_IV_TREND` | `high_iv_sell_call` (income style) | `SELL_COVERED_CALL_PARTIAL` | N/A | no |
| 06 | `06-low-iv-range-wheel-short-put` | `LOW_IV_RANGE` | `wheel_on_low_iv_range` | `WHEEL_SHORT_PUT` | N/A | no |
| 07 | `07-breakout-monetize-put` | `BREAKOUT` | `monetize_put_on_breakout` | `MONETIZE_PUT` | N/A | no |
| 08 | `08-breakout-reduce-coverage-post-event` | `BREAKOUT` | `reduce_coverage_on_breakout_post_event` | `REDUCE_COVERAGE` | N/A | no |
| 09 | `09-post-event-reprice-collar` | `POST_EVENT_REPRICE` | `open_collar_pre_event` | `OPEN_COLLAR` | structure present | no |
| 10 | `10-roll-up-and-out-threatened-short-call` | any (rule triggers on position state, not regime) | `roll_up_and_out_when_short_call_threatened` | `ROLL_UP_AND_OUT` | N/A | no |
| 11 | `11-hold-no-op-low-confidence` | any | `hold_no_op` | `NO_OP` | N/A | no |
| 12 | `12-illiquid-chain-ladder-escalated` | healthy (e.g. `HIGH_IV_PIN`) | non-collar (e.g. `high_iv_sell_call`) | varies | N/A | **escalated=True** |

## Status (as of M1.24 implementation PR)

- ✅ Fixtures 01, 03, 11 — `inputs.json` authored.
- ⏳ Fixtures 02, 04, 05, 06, 07, 08, 09, 10, 12 — scenario README only; `inputs.json` to be authored in follow-up commits before the PR flips to ready-for-review.
- ⏳ All `expected.json` files — regenerate via the script above after every fixture's `inputs.json` is authored. The harness emits a clear "run regen" failure message until then.

## Determinism notes

- Floats in `expected.json` are rounded to 6 decimal places by the canonical
  serializer (`engine.decision.serialize_canonical`). Max precision loss is
  5e-7, two orders of magnitude below the UI display precision (2 places).
- Naive `datetime`s are assumed UTC, matching `compute_inputs_hash`.
- Tuples become lists; frozensets become sorted lists (by `repr`).
- Pydantic models serialize via `model_dump(mode="json")`.

See the canonical serializer's module docstring at
`packages/engine/engine/decision/serialize.py` for the full canonicalization
contract.
